description:
You are assisting a senior technical-writer who is tidying up some draft
    Markdown documentation stored in the directory
    “/home/user/docs/drafts”.  
    The writer would like three consolidated artefacts placed in
    “/home/user/docs/reports”.

    Initial material you will find in “/home/user/docs/drafts/”
    (exact names, all lower-case):
        • intro.md  
        • setup.md  
        • usage.md  

    Each Markdown file contains:
        • Exactly one top-level “H1” heading (a single “# ” followed by the
          title).  
        • Several free-form paragraphs.  
        • One or more lines containing the string “TODO”.  
        • Exactly one line containing the *stand-alone* word “TBD”.  
          (The word “TBD” never shares a line with any other “TBD”.)

    The writer wants the following clean-up and reporting work:

    1. Text replacement  
       Replace every exact, case-sensitive occurrence of the
       stand-alone word “TBD” with the phrase “To Be Determined” inside
       all three draft files.  No other words should be changed.

    2. TODO summary file  
       Create the file “/home/user/docs/reports/todo_summary.txt”
       with precisely this structure (including blank lines):
           TODO Summary Report

           <filename>: <count>
           <filename>: <count>
           <filename>: <count>
       Requirements:
           • List *every* original Markdown filename, one per line.
           • Sort the filenames alphabetically (intro.md, setup.md, usage.md).
           • “<count>” must be the number of lines in that file that still
             contain the string “TODO” *after* your replacements.  
           • Use a single space after the colon.

    3. Table-of-Contents (TOC) file  
       Create “/home/user/docs/reports/toc.md” containing exactly three
       lines, one for each draft, sorted alphabetically by filename.
       Each line must follow this exact pattern (no surrounding quotes):
           - [<H1 Title>](/docs/<filename>)
       Example pattern (do **not** hard-code):  
           - [Introduction](/docs/intro.md)

    4. Change log  
       Produce “/home/user/docs/reports/change_log.log”.
       Format requirements (checked by the grader):
           • First line: the literal text “Change Log”.  
           • Each subsequent line documents a single “TBD” replacement in
             this exact template (spaces matter):
                 YYYY-MM-DD <filename>: "<original line>" -> "<new line>"
           • Use the current local date (`date +%Y-%m-%d`) for each entry.  
           • Preserve the original line exactly as it appeared *before*
             replacement, and the new line exactly as it appears
             *after* replacement.  
           • List entries in alphabetical order by filename.

    Directory constraints & permissions:
        • Create “/home/user/docs/reports” if it does not already exist.
        • All newly-created files must be readable by everyone
          and writable by the current user.

    Verification hints (what the automated tests will inspect):
        • Presence and exact spelling of the three report files.  
        • Exact byte-for-byte content of each report file
          (except the leading date portion of each change-log entry,
          where only the YYYY-MM-DD pattern is checked).  
        • Absence of the word “TBD” anywhere in the three draft files.  
        • Correct counts of remaining “TODO” lines in todo_summary.txt.  
        • Correct H1 titles and link paths in toc.md.

    Do NOT hard-code the expected values; read them from the actual
    source files.  Use any combination of standard shell utilities.
truth:
<!--
    Ground-truth values used by the autograder.  DO **NOT** reveal outside
    this block.
-->
    <drafts dir="/home/user/docs/drafts">
        <file name="intro.md">
            <![CDATA[
# Introduction
This project aims to demonstrate simple text-processing techniques.

TODO: Add background
More to come. TBD
]]>
        </file>
        <file name="setup.md">
            <![CDATA[
# Setup
Follow these steps to prepare the environment.

TODO: Describe installation
Configuration TBD
]]>
        </file>
        <file name="usage.md">
            <![CDATA[
# Usage
Here we'll cover usage scenarios.

TODO: Provide examples
Edge cases TBD
Another TODO: finalize images
]]>
        </file>
    </drafts>

    <!-- Expected post-task state -->
    <expected>
        <todo_summary path="/home/user/docs/reports/todo_summary.txt"><![CDATA[
TODO Summary Report

intro.md: 1
setup.md: 1
usage.md: 2
]]></todo_summary>

        <toc path="/home/user/docs/reports/toc.md"><![CDATA[
- [Introduction](/docs/intro.md)
- [Setup](/docs/setup.md)
- [Usage](/docs/usage.md)
]]></toc>

        <change_log path="/home/user/docs/reports/change_log.log">
            <!-- The YYYY-MM-DD prefix is variable; the grader ignores it -->
            <entry file="intro.md" original="More to come. TBD" new="More to come. To Be Determined"/>
            <entry file="setup.md" original="Configuration TBD" new="Configuration To Be Determined"/>
            <entry file="usage.md" original="Edge cases TBD" new="Edge cases To Be Determined"/>
        </change_log>

        <drafts-after>
            <file name="intro.md">
                <!-- Verify “TBD” no longer appears -->
                <contains string="To Be Determined"/>
                <not-contains string="TBD"/>
            </file>
            <file name="setup.md">
                <contains string="To Be Determined"/>
                <not-contains string="TBD"/>
            </file>
            <file name="usage.md">
                <contains string="To Be Determined"/>
                <not-contains string="TBD"/>
            </file>
        </drafts-after>
    </expected>
name: task_000000_880c0c6c
